Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows

New report from Aerospace Corp. highlights rising EU funding and defense focus...

European aerospace competitiveness increasingly depends on coordinating civil space investments with defense spending priorities to compete with U.S. and Chinese capabilities.

See SpaceNews →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

DroneShield opens Amsterdam HQ as counter-drone demand grows

Drone threats aren’t slowing down in Europe, and neither is DroneShield. The Australia-based counter-drone and electronic warfare specialist has officially opened its new European headquarters in Amsterdam, signaling a deeper, more permanent push into one of its fastest-growing markets. more…...

DroneShield's European expansion reflects institutional recognition that counter-drone technology is now mission-critical infrastructure rather than emerging capability.

See DroneDJ →

Military Mar 31, 2026

Embraer KC-390 Millennium showcases cold weather operations in Sweden

Embraer’s KC-390 Millennium demonstrator has completed short takeoff and landing operations at Sweden’s Vidsel Test Range as part of the…...

KC-390 Millennium's Swedish trials demonstrate tactical airlifters can operate from austere, frozen airfields, expanding deployment options for allied air forces in northern Europe.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Sphere ditches contractors, speeds up HubT drone deployments

Sphere is tightening its grip on autonomous drone deployments with a set of practical upgrades to its HubT platform, but the biggest shift is how the system is now built. more…...

Sphere's vertical integration of HubT drone operations by eliminating contractor dependencies reduces deployment timelines and operational complexity while improving quality control over autonomous systems.

See DroneDJ →

PAI
Regulatory Mar 31, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

Operators must formally document their safety management system alignment with FAA standards, creating auditable evidence of compliance that regulators will scrutinize during inspections.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Researchers say SAF made from solar energy could be scaled to industrial levels

The University of Sheffield has developed a new way of making sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) using solar energy…...

Solar-powered SAF production could reduce aviation's dependence on fossil fuels while lowering manufacturing costs through direct renewable energy integration at scale.

See AeroTime Hub →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

UK drone rules just got way easier to understand

Grey Arrows Drone Club just did something UK drone pilots have quietly needed for years. And if you’ve ever second-guessed where you’re allowed to take off, this could be a game-changer. more…...

Simplified regulatory guidance reduces operational ambiguity that has previously deterred recreational and commercial drone adoption in the UK market.

See DroneDJ →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

The 5 Airlines With The Largest Boeing 737 Fleets Today

Boeing's single-aisle narrowbody family has been a popular choice for many years.

The 737's dominance in major carrier fleets underscores both Boeing's market penetration and the operational risk concentration if supply-chain disruptions or safety issues emerge.

Military Mar 31, 2026

Rheinmetall and Boeing Australia formalize MQ-28 Ghost Bat bid

Germany’s interest in acquiring the MQ-28 Ghost Bat has moved from diplomatic signals to a formal industrial partnership in a…...

Germany's formal partnership with Boeing signals NATO's accelerating adoption of autonomous combat drones, expanding the MQ-28's export potential beyond traditional allies.

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document why residual risks remain acceptable after implementing controls, creating defensible safety decisions under regulatory scrutiny.

Drones Mar 31, 2026

MiniV-Bat 234g, 74 minutes hover bicopter

Bicopters are theoretically the most efficient multi-rotor design for their size constraint, yet their real-world...

Bicopters' theoretical efficiency advantage over quadcopters becomes practically viable at small scales, enabling extended mission endurance for micro-drone applications.

See sUAS News →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

2026 might be the most affordable year to buy a DJI drone

DJI drones that once felt like serious investments are now showing up at prices that would’ve seemed unrealistic even a year ago. Thanks to the Amazon Big Spring Sale, models across the lineup are seeing steep discounts, opening the door at just $149 and making it very easy to imagine how different...

Competitive pricing pressure from major retailers could accelerate consumer drone adoption and shift market dynamics from premium positioning to mass-market accessibility.

See DroneDJ →

Drones Mar 31, 2026

Drone surveying without GCPs? Wingtra says it’s here

Wingtra is stepping into 2026 with a clear message for the surveying world: the future of mapping might not need ground control points at all. The Switzerland-based drone company recently launched the SURVEY61 payload, designed to work with the WingtraRAY drone. And the pitch is hard to ignore —...

Wingtra's new SURVEY61 payload eliminates the need for ground control points in drone surveying, potentially reducing field setup time and costs while accelerating project timelines for surveying professionals.

See DroneDJ →

Business Aviation Mar 31, 2026

Sikorsky Collaborates with Robinson Helicopter Company to Integrate MATRIX™ Autonomy into Robinson R66 TURBINETRUCK

Sikorsky, a Lockheed Martin company (NYSE:LMT), and Robinson Helicopter Company (RHC) unveiled the R66 TURBINETRUCK, a game-changing...

Sikorsky's autonomy technology integration into a turbine-powered Robinson helicopter demonstrates commercial viability of autonomous systems in mid-size rotorcraft beyond military applications.

See sUAS News →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Surface Safety Spotlight – Los Angeles International Airport, CA (LAX) – From the Flight Deck

Los Angeles International Airport (LAX) is one of the nation’s busiest commercial airports. This short video highlights a runway incursion hazard where pilots exiting Runway 24R, particularly via...

Runway incursion hazards at high-traffic airports like LAX require pilots to execute precise exit procedures, as confusion during post-landing taxi operations directly impacts gate efficiency and safety margins.

See Federal Aviation Administration (YouTube) →

Aerospace Mar 31, 2026

Artemis 2 countdown underway

The two-day countdown for the Artemis 2 mission around the moon started March 30 with NASA officials reporting no major issues for the launch.

NASA's Artemis 2 lunar mission represents the critical validation flight for human deep-space exploration infrastructure before crewed lunar surface operations resume.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 31, 2026

ICE Eyes 10 New Boeing 737 And Gulfstream G650s For Deportation Fleet

The controversial agency is jet shopping again.

ICE's expansion of its deportation aircraft fleet signals sustained federal investment in immigration enforcement infrastructure despite ongoing public scrutiny of the program's operations.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

Inside The Boeing 747's Upper Deck Layout

From a secluded cocktail bar to a high-density seating zone, the evolution of the 747's upper deck reflects the changing priorities of aviation.

The 747's upper deck evolution from luxury lounge to high-density seating mirrors airlines' shift from premium experience differentiation to capacity monetization strategies.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 31, 2026

Fires Tear Through Nebraska Grasslands

Dry, warm, and windy conditions across the U.S. Great Plains led to extreme fire activity in March 2026.

Extreme grassland fire conditions in March 2026 could significantly impact low-altitude operations and visibility for general aviation aircraft operating across the Great Plains region.

The San Francisco Bay area is complicated airspace, and right in the middle of it is San Carlos Airport. San Carlos airport is surrounded by terrain, San Francisco International class bravo,...

San Carlos Airport's noise abatement procedures must balance operational efficiency with residential constraints inherent to Bay Area terrain and proximity to Class B airspace.

Military Mar 31, 2026

5 Fighter Jets With The Highest Top Speeds In Service Today

From the Mach 2.83 MiG-31 to the F-22, these are the 5 fastest fighter jets still in service—and why speed still matters in modern air combat.

Despite advanced sensor and missile technology reducing traditional dogfighting, sustained supersonic speed remains operationally critical for interception, energy management, and tactical advantage in contested airspace.

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

JetBlue Hikes Checked Bags Fees, Cites "Rising Operating Costs"

JetBlue announces a hike checked bag fees, balancing the need to keep fares competitive with the reality of escalating operations costs.

JetBlue's fee increase reflects industry-wide pressure to offset fuel and labor costs without pricing passengers out of the market entirely.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 31, 2026

5 Routes That Push Aircraft To Their Range Limits

These services offer extra-long-range connectivity.

Ultra-long-range routes drive demand for specialized aircraft with higher operating costs, creating a niche market segment that airlines must carefully balance against fuel efficiency and crew fatigue regulations.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Second Starlink satellite suffers anomaly, generating debris

For the second time in just over three months, a SpaceX Starlink satellite has generated debris from an apparent on-orbit malfunction.

Recurring Starlink anomalies raise collision risk concerns for LEO constellations and highlight the critical need for improved satellite reliability and debris mitigation protocols.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Mar 31, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation sectors offers near-term emissions reductions without waiting for new aircraft technology, making efficiency improvements a critical interim sustainability strategy.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta Slashes Domestic Boeing 767-400 Flights By 67% In Massive Schedule Shakeup

The airline will redeploy a significant portion of its 767-400ER fleet from domestic routes to services to and from Europe, affecting several routes.

Delta's redeployment signals that widebody aircraft economics favor international routes over domestic capacity, reshaping transcontinental travel supply.

See Simple Flying →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Inside The F-35's Cockpit System

At the controls of flying supercomputer.

The F-35's integrated avionics architecture consolidates sensor fusion, threat detection, and weapon management into a unified cockpit interface that fundamentally changes pilot workload and decision-making paradigms.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

TSA Employees Asked To Return Tyler Perry’s $1,000 Gift Cards As Backpay Begins

TSA workers receiving backpay as shutdown enters day 45, but those who received $1,000 gift cards from Tyler Perry are asked to return them.

The TSA's backpay distribution during the shutdown reveals operational challenges in managing employee benefits and third-party donations, which airlines depend on for secure airport operations.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

London Gatwick Confirms 9th New Airline In Massive 2026 Shakeup

Pegasus gives Gatwick a fresh Istanbul link as its surprising 2026 growth surges.

Gatwick's capacity expansion is attracting point-to-point carriers seeking alternatives to congested London hubs, signaling a strategic shift in UK airport competitive dynamics.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Rocket Lab wins German approval for Mynaric deal

Decision clears path for April closing after months of scrutiny over keeping key space technology in domestic hands...

German regulatory approval removes a major geopolitical barrier to Rocket Lab's acquisition of optical communication technology, reducing risk for aerospace suppliers dependent on cross-border consolidation.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Video: Delta A330 returns to São Paulo after engine failure on takeoff

A Delta Air Lines Airbus A330-300 returned to São Paulo Guarulhos Airport shortly after takeoff on March 29,…...

Engine failures during takeoff demand immediate return decisions, testing crew coordination and aircraft handling in critical phases where recovery options are most constrained.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Artemis II by the numbers: an inside look at mankind’s planned return to the Moon

NASA’s Artemis II astronauts are go for launch, with the agency targeting April 1, 2026, at 18:24 local…...

Artemis II's April 2026 timeline demonstrates NASA's commitment to lunar return infrastructure that will eventually support sustained human presence and commercial space station operations in cislunar space.

Military Mar 30, 2026

China’s Y-9FQ Next Gen ASW Aircraft Intercepted for the First Time Over East China Sea

The Y-9FQ ‘High New 15’ made its debut at the Sep. 3, 2025, Victory Day parade in Beijing, and has now been intercepted for the first time by Japan. A Chinese People’s Liberation Army Naval Air Force (PLANAF) Y-9FQ maritime patrol and anti-submarine warfare aircraft was intercepted for the first...

China's operational deployment of the Y-9FQ ASW aircraft in contested airspace signals accelerated modernization of regional maritime patrol capabilities with potential implications for allied air defense interception protocols.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

American’s BBQ partnership with Pecan Lodge hits the spot

Some airline routes are special because of the insatiable passenger demand they create, like Seoul to Jeju in South Korea, which is often ranked as the world’s busiest route. Others are special because they link two globally influential cities like New York and London. Then there’s Dallas to New...

Airlines are leveraging local culinary partnerships to differentiate route experiences and build brand loyalty on competitive domestic corridors beyond traditional hub-and-spoke economics.

Rhea Space, Sandia and NASA hardware to gather data during high-speed descent...

NORAD Escorts Aircraft After Palm Beach TFR Violation

F-16s intercepted the general aviation aircraft near Mar-a-Lago on Sunday afternoon.

TFR violations near high-security areas now trigger immediate military interception, underscoring escalating enforcement of airspace restrictions around protected facilities.
